findings.title: Path Traversal in Backup Restore Endpoint
findings.description: The restore_backup endpoint takes a user-supplied filename and joins it directly with BACKUP_DIR using os.path.join without any sanitization. An attacker can supply path traversal sequences to read arbitrary JSON files.

findings.title: Wildcard CORS Policy with Credentials Allowed
findings.description: CORS middleware configured with allow_origins=['*'], allow_credentials=True. Any origin can make cross-origin requests.
findings.fix: Restrict allow_origins to specific trusted domains. Set allow_credentials=False.

findings.title: No Authentication or Authorization on Any Endpoint
findings.description: The entire API has no authentication. All endpoints including destructive operations are publicly accessible.
findings.evidence: No auth middleware, no Depends() for authentication, no API key checks
findings.fix: Implement JWT/OAuth2/API key authentication as a FastAPI dependency.

findings.title: No CSRF Protection on State-Changing Endpoints
findings.description: State-changing endpoints (POST/PUT/DELETE) have no CSRF protection. Combined with permissive CORS, malicious sites could forge requests.
findings.evidence: No CSRF tokens in frontend or backend
findings.fix: Implement CSRF tokens or SameSite cookie policy.

findings.title: Unvalidated Data in Backup Import Endpoint
findings.description: Import endpoint accepts raw dict with no Pydantic validation, bypassing all schema checks enforced on normal CRUD.
findings.evidence: def import_data(data: dict): -- raw dict, no model
findings.fix: Define a Pydantic model for the import payload.

findings.title: Unvalidated Data in Backup Restore Endpoint
findings.description: Restore reads JSON from disk and inserts into DB without validation. Tampered backup files inject arbitrary data.
findings.evidence: backup = json.load(f); direct insert without validation
findings.fix: Validate restored data against Pydantic schemas. Sign backup files with HMAC.

findings.title: Destructive Operations Without Transaction Safety
findings.description: Restore/import DELETE all data before inserting replacements. If inserts fail partway through, data is lost.
findings.evidence: DELETE FROM transactions; DELETE FROM recurring_transactions; then inserts that may fail
findings.fix: Wrap in proper transaction with rollback on failure.

findings.title: Dynamic SQL Column Names from Model Dump
findings.description: Update endpoint uses f-string interpolation for column names from model_dump(). Safe via Pydantic but risky pattern.
findings.fix: Add explicit allowlist of permitted column names.

findings.title: Backup Files Stored Without Encryption
findings.description: Backup files with financial data written as plaintext JSON with default permissions.
findings.fix: Set restrictive permissions (0o700) and consider encryption.

findings.title: No Security Headers Configured
findings.description: No CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, HSTS, or Referrer-Policy headers set.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on API Endpoints
findings.description: No rate limiting. Attacker can flood endpoints or exhaust disk via backup creation.
findings.fix: Add slowapi or custom rate limiting middleware.

findings.title: No Validation on generate_transactions Date Parameters
findings.description: Date query params not validated. Wide date range could generate massive number of transactions (DoS).
findings.evidence: start_date: str = Query(...) -- no format or range validation
findings.fix: Add date format validation and max range limit.

findings.title: Sensitive Data Exposure via Error Messages
findings.description: FastAPI returns detailed tracebacks on unhandled exceptions, exposing internal paths and versions.
findings.fix: Add global exception handler returning generic error messages.

findings.title: No Logging or Audit Trail
findings.description: No logging for security events. Impossible to detect or investigate incidents (OWASP A09).
findings.evidence: No import logging, no logger calls in any source file
findings.fix: Configure Python logging module with structured logging.

findings.title: Database File World-Accessible
findings.description: SQLite DB created with default permissions, potentially readable by other users.

findings.title: Frontend Passes Raw User Input into Restore URL
findings.description: showRestoreDialog() uses raw prompt() input in fetch URL, enabling client-side path traversal.
findings.fix: Validate input matches listed filenames. Use dropdown instead of free text.

summary: SAST review found 16 issues: 1 critical (path traversal), 4 high (no auth, CORS wildcard, unvalidated import/restore), 7 medium, 2 low. SQL injection properly mitigated via parameterized queries. Frontend XSS mitigated via escapeHtml().
